event: albuminocytologic dissociation in the cerebrospinal fluid
brain magnetic resonance imaging showed small vessel ischemic changes
no clinical response to intravenous immunoglobulin (IVIG)
flaccid paralysis of all four extremities
provisional diagnosis of acute inflammatory demyelinating polyradiculoneuropathy (AIDP)
high-dose intravenous thiamine 500 mg every 8 hours commenced
admitted to the intensive care unit
electroencephalogram (EEG) did not show seizure activity
brain magnetic resonance imaging showed hyperintensity of the bilateral medial thalamus
transferred out of the intensive care unit
